tests.subject: Biology
tests.question: Explain the function of sucrose in the preparation of a pollen grain slide.
tests.studentAnswer: It provides nutrients and energy for the pollen grain to germinate and grow a pollen tube.
tests.notes: Should not penalise pollen tube wording; no liver topic leakage.

tests.subject: Chemistry
tests.question: Give an example of a composite material and describe one of its uses.
tests.studentAnswer: Reinforced concrete is a composite material. It is used to construct buildings and bridges.
tests.notes: Max marks 2; alternative composite must be accepted.

tests.subject: Chemistry
tests.question: What is the primary purpose of using personal protective equipment (PPE) in a chemistry laboratory?
tests.studentAnswer: PPE is used to protect us from chemicals and accidents in the laboratory.
tests.notes: General PPE purpose; do not require naming goggles only.

tests.subject: Biology
tests.question: Explain the effect of temperature on the rate of photosynthesis using Hydrilla sp.
tests.studentAnswer: When temperature increases, Hydrilla photosynthesises faster until the optimum temperature. After that, the rate decreases because enzymes are denatured. More oxygen bubbles show a higher rate of photosynthesis.

tests.subject: Biology
tests.question: Explain why a 100-meter sprinter breathes rapidly for a few minutes after completing the race.
tests.studentAnswer: Because the body needs more oxygen after running.
tests.notes: Partial only; should still list lactic acid / oxygen debt as gaps.

tests.subject: Biology
tests.question: Describe the structure of a sperm cell.
tests.studentAnswer: A sperm cell has a head, middle part and tail. The tail helps it move.
tests.notes: Partial credit; structure describe should not force causal links for every label.

tests.subject: Biology
tests.question: Why is the ABO blood group system considered an example of multiple alleles and not just two alleles like in Mendel’s pea plant experiments?
tests.studentAnswer: Because ABO blood group has three types of alleles, which are IA, IB and IO. A person only carries two alleles, but in the population there are more than two alleles. That is why ABO blood group is called multiple alleles.
tests.notes: Mostly correct; do not mark too harshly for Mendel comparison wording.

tests.subject: Chemistry
tests.question: Why are ceramics brittle and easily broken when hit?
tests.studentAnswer: Because their particles are held in a fixed arrangement and cannot slide over each other easily.
tests.notes: May still want crack propagation idea as partial gap.

tests.subject: Chemistry
tests.question: State two properties of composite materials.
tests.studentAnswer: Composite materials have high strength and low weight.

tests.subject: Chemistry
tests.question: Describe the evolution of atomic models from John Dalton to Niels Bohr.
tests.studentAnswer: Dalton said atoms are solid spheres. Thomson discovered electrons. Rutherford found the nucleus, and Bohr said electrons move in shells.
tests.notes: Brief but substantial; should not auto-full without detail.

tests.subject: Chemistry
tests.question: Explain why the rate of reaction increases when temperature increases. (3 marks)
tests.studentAnswer: Particles move faster and collide more often. More particles have enough energy to overcome activation energy.
tests.notes: Elliptical but correct SPM cause-effect answer; do not require repeated because/so that wording.

tests.subject: Chemistry
tests.question: Explain why the rate of reaction increases when temperature increases. (3 marks)
tests.studentAnswer: Particles move faster and collide more often. More particles have enough energy to overcome activation energy.
tests.notes: Test A: full causal SPM answer without because/therefore — must score 3/3.

tests.subject: Chemistry
tests.question: Explain why powdered calcium carbonate reacts faster than marble chips with hydrochloric acid. (3 marks)
tests.studentAnswer: Powdered calcium carbonate has a larger surface area. More particles are exposed to the acid, so there are more frequent collisions.
tests.notes: Test B: surface area / exposure / collisions — must score 3/3.

tests.subject: Biology
tests.question: Explain the function of phloem tissue in plants. (2 marks)
tests.notes: Generic transport only — 1/2; route (from leaves to other parts) must stay missing.
